sections.paragraph: βέβηλος, ον [cp. βαίνω ‘walk’ and βῆλος ‘theshhold’; from the idea of being beyond the threshold and therefore in public area, esp. not dedicated to religious use, develops the mng. ‘accessible (to anyone), profane’; cp. Lat. profanus] ‘not in accord with a sense of what is sacred’, of pers. irreverent, worldly, profane 1 Ti 1:9; Hb 12:16; of verbal utterances trivial, profane 1 Ti 4:7; 6:20; 2 Ti 2:16.
